contextSummary: Cloudflare's official component library. Field takes a single flat prop set (label, required, labelTooltip, error, description, controlFirst, hideLabel) instead of exposing Field.Label/Error/Description as JSX children, and documents a hideLabel escape hatch for controls like Select that supply their own accessible label.
rankRationale: The richest single teaching example: a corporate design system that collapses the entire Field composition into one flat prop API (label/required/labelTooltip/error/description/controlFirst/hideLabel) rather than exposing Field.Label/Error/Description as children, with an explicit `hideLabel` escape hatch whose JSDoc explains exactly when a native `<label>` is the wrong choice (controls like Select that already provide their own accessible label, to avoid hover/focus coupling). Also documents a horizontal 'controlFirst' layout for checkbox/switch-style fields via `has-[...]` selectors rather than a separate component variant. A recomposed story would contrast the vertical/horizontal layouts and the hideLabel case side by side. Feeds: prop-level guidance, the a11y contract (native-label pitfalls), and the composition-vs-flat-prop-API design-decision discussion.

contextSummary: France's national statistics institute questionnaire designer. Its Field wrapper forwards dirty/touched/invalid/name straight through from a react-hook-form FieldError object into Field.Root/Field.Error's match prop.
rankRationale: The clearest first-party example of bridging Base UI Field's own validation-state model (`dirty`, `touched`, `invalid` props on Field.Root) with an external form library's error object: its `Props.error` is typed as react-hook-form's own `FieldError`, and the JSDoc'd component signature (`invalid`, `touched`, `dirty` all individually documented with cross-references to `BaseUIField.Root.Props`) is exactly the kind of integration question real consumers ask. A recomposed story is 'Field driven by an external validation library' — pass dirty/touched/invalid from a mock RHF-shaped state and watch Field.Error toggle. Feeds: the react-hook-form / external-state integration section, and the dirty/touched/invalid prop reference.

contextSummary: WordPress' new @wordpress/ui form-primitives package wraps Field one file per part; Root's JSDoc says to use Fieldset instead for labeling groups of controls, and warns wrapping alone doesn't guarantee accessible labeling.
rankRationale: WordPress' framework-scale @wordpress/ui package decomposes Field one file per part, matching the same convention documented for its Select/Autocomplete/Combobox siblings, and its Root JSDoc is unusually explicit about the a11y contract: 'simply wrapping a control with this component does not guarantee accessible labeling' plus a direct pointer to Fieldset for multi-control groups. Feeds: the a11y contract section (labeling isn't automatic) and Field-vs-Fieldset boundary guidance.

contextSummary: Typebot exposes Field as a namespace object with a Container sub-part (bordered card grouping) and an error-deduplication helper that collapses repeated messages into one line or a bulleted list.
rankRationale: Typebot (an open-source chatbot/form builder, 10k stars) adds a `Field.Container` sub-part with no Base UI equivalent — a bordered card wrapper for visually grouping a field, distinct from Fieldset's semantic-legend grouping — and a reusable error-deduplication helper (also independently found, near-verbatim, in dify's Field usage and lumi-ui's shadcn-style siblings) that collapses repeated validation messages into a single line or bulleted list. A recomposed story shows the Container variant and the multi-error bulleted-list case. Feeds: layout variations beyond the default vertical stack, and the multiple-simultaneous-errors display pattern.

contextSummary: Apsara, Raystack/Gojek's design system. Styled with CSS Modules rather than Tailwind, and shares invalid/disabled/required state across sibling parts via a React context provider rather than relying only on Base UI's data-attribute cascade.
rankRationale: The only Field wrapper in the corpus styled with CSS Modules rather than Tailwind — useful counter-evidence that Base UI composes cleanly with any styling approach — and the only one that layers its own React context (FieldContext carrying invalid/disabled/required) alongside Base UI's own data-attribute state, presumably so sibling custom parts (like a separately-rendered required-asterisk) can read field state without re-deriving it from data attributes. A recomposed story is the CSS-Modules-styled Field with a custom part reading FieldContext. Feeds: the styling-approach-agnostic message and advanced-composition (sharing field state to non-Base-UI children).

contextSummary: Selia's FieldItem uses a grid-cols-[auto_1fr] layout with the description column-start-2, so helper/error text aligns under the control rather than the label — a distinct layout convention from the vertical-stack default.
rankRationale: Selia's FieldItem is the only wrapper using a CSS-grid `[auto_1fr]` layout with the description/error text explicitly pinned to the second column so it aligns under the control instead of the label — a concrete alternative to the vertical-stack layout almost every other candidate uses. Feeds: layout-variation guidance (grid vs stack) and the live-site real-world example.

contextSummary: Kaneo, an open-source project-management tool. A standard thin data-slot Field wrapper in its docs/marketing site UI kit.
rankRationale: Plain, unremarkable production evidence — a thin data-slot Field wrapper in an ordinary open-source SaaS app's UI kit, not a design-system registry. Its value is precisely that it's ordinary: it shows the shadcn-for-Base-UI Field pattern has propagated past design-system authors into everyday app codebases with no further customization needed. Feeds: the 'this pattern travels well' real-world-adoption framing.

contextSummary: Green-Wall (a GitHub contribution-wall image generator) still imports from the pre-1.0 @base-ui-components/react/field specifier rather than @base-ui/react/field.
rankRationale: A recently-updated (2026-06), actively-maintained app still importing from the retired `@base-ui-components/react/field` specifier — concrete, dated evidence that package-rename migration lags in real codebases, useful for a migration-guide callout ('if you see this import, it predates the 1.0 rename') rather than for illustrating Field usage itself, which is otherwise a standard thin wrapper. Feeds: the migration/versioning notes and package-rename historical record.

contextSummary: lumi-ui's Field.Control forwards cva inputVariants (size/variant) so a bare FieldControl renders as a styled input, and FieldLabel auto-appends a * via group-has-required/aria-required pseudo-classes instead of manual markup.
rankRationale: FieldControl directly forwards cva `inputVariants` so a bare `<FieldControl inputSize="sm">` renders pre-styled without extra composition, and FieldLabel derives its required-asterisk purely from CSS (`group-has-required:after:content-['*']`, `aria-required:after:content-['*']`) rather than a JS-computed indicator — a cleaner alternative to manually rendering a `*` span. Feeds: the required-field-indicator pattern and cva-integration guidance.

contextSummary: Cal.com's public design-system registry. Thin data-slot passthrough for every Field part including Control and Validity.
rankRationale: A standard thin data-slot wrapper, but from Cal.com's public design-system registry (10.2k stars) — a recognizable brand credibility anchor for the real-world examples list even though the wrapper itself covers ground already shown by kumo/lumi-ui/kaneo. Feeds: the real-world-examples header citation list.
